items.summary: Roof is reserved to Landlord and excluded from the demise, and if Tenant's own repairs require roof access, Landlord performs the work but Tenant reimburses all costs plus an administrative fee.
items.quote: if any such repairs or replacements described in the foregoing sentence necessitate entry into or onto the roof of the Premises, the Common Areas, or any other tenant's premises, Landlord shall be responsible for any such maintenance, repair, and/or replacements to be made, but Tenant shall be responsible for all costs incurred by Landlord in conjunction therewith

items.summary: Landlord reserves the roof and exterior/structural walls to itself and is not liable for damages from defects in the building's structure absent its negligence; structural repairs fall outside Tenant's nonstructural repair obligation.
items.quote: Landlord shall not be liable for damages caused by, or growing out of, any defect in the structure of the building; breakage, leakage, service interruption; or defective condition of the electric wiring, air conditioning and heating equipment, closets, or plumbing not directly caused by Landlord's negligence or failure to cure.

items.summary: Landlord controls and may alter, maintain or repair utility lines, HVAC, plumbing and other systems within the Common Areas and Shopping Center, and Tenant only has a nonexclusive right to use them.
items.quote: Landlord may from time to time: (i) vertically and horizontally locate, alter, maintain or repair utility lines, air ducts, flues, duct shafts, sprinkler main and valves, plumbing, HVAC and other systems, and/or facilities through or within the Premises...or other parts of the Shopping Center

items.summary: Tenant must perform all nonstructural repairs, maintenance and alterations not specifically made Landlord's obligation, as well as repairs needed due to Tenant's or its agents' acts, occupancy, or break-ins.
items.quote: Tenant shall perform all nonstructural repairs, maintenance and Alterations not specifically set forth in Section 6.2 as an obligation of Landlord and all repairs and maintenance described in Article 5 and elsewhere throughout the Lease.

items.summary: Tenant must make or perform any maintenance, repairs or replacements caused by its own or its agents' acts or neglect, its occupancy, or break-ins/burglary.
items.quote: Tenant shall make or perform any maintenance, repairs or replacements occasioned by the act or neglect of Tenant, its permitted assignees, sublessees, servants, agents, employees, licensees, or concessionaires, or any damage caused by or as a result of Tenant's occupancy of Premises, or any damage caused by break-in, burglary, or other similar acts inside, to or into the Premises

items.summary: Tenant is directly and solely responsible, at its own cost, for obtaining and maintaining refuse/trash collection service for the Premises (unless Landlord elects to provide it, in which case Tenant pays its share).
items.quote: Tenant acknowledges and agrees that it shall be directly and solely responsible, at its sole cost and expense, for obtaining and maintaining a contract with an approved refuse collection company...for the removal of waste and garbage from the Premises

items.summary: For ADA compliance, Tenant is responsible for the interior/non-structural portions of the Premises while Landlord is responsible for the exterior/structural aspects of the Premises and the Common Areas; if Common Area ADA work is triggered by Tenant's alterations/use, Landlord performs it but Tenant pays the cost.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain the interior, non-structural portions of the Premises in compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act at its sole expense, and Landlord shall be responsible for the compliance of the exterior, structural aspects of the Premises and the Common Areas of the Shopping Center with the Americans with Disabilities Act

items.summary: Tenant maintains, repairs and replaces the interior and non-structural elements of the Premises at its own cost.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain, repair, and replace, at its sole cost and expense (except as otherwise stated herein), the interior of and exterior non-structural portions of the Premises, including the entire non-structural exterior storefront, trade dress and signage, plate glass, and all doors, ceilings, walls, and windows of the Premises

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for the non-structural storefront, trade dress, signage, plate glass, and doors.
items.quote: including the entire non-structural exterior storefront, trade dress and signage, plate glass, and all doors, ceilings, walls, and windows of the Premises

items.summary: Tenant maintains, repairs, and replaces all electrical systems serving the Premises.
items.quote: and all electrical, plumbing (including free flow up to the connection of the main sewer line), and all other utility lines, facilities and systems

items.summary: Tenant maintains, repairs, and replaces plumbing serving the Premises, including free flow up to the main sewer line connection.
items.quote: and all electrical, plumbing (including free flow up to the connection of the main sewer line), and all other utility lines, facilities and systems

items.summary: Tenant maintains, repairs, and replaces the fire sprinkler system serving the Premises.
items.quote: heating, ventilating, air conditioning system (âHVAC Systemâ), fire sprinkler system, and any other mechanical installations, either exclusively serving the Premises or from the stub into the Premises

items.summary: Tenant is solely responsible for maintaining, repairing, and (after warranty) replacing the HVAC/RTU under a required service contract; however, during the RTU Warranty Period, Landlord pays for RTU repairs beyond routine maintenance at Landlord's expense.
items.quote: Tenant shall be solely responsible for the maintenance, repair and replacement of the HVAC System (including, without limitation, the RTU) in accordance with the terms of Section 6.1 above and Section 6.2(d) below... in the event the RTU requires repairs beyond routine inspections and maintenance during the RTU Warranty Period, Landlord shall, at Landlordâs expense, make such necessary repairs to the RTU

items.summary: Landlord performs any repair/maintenance work requiring entry onto the roof (other than HVAC work), but Tenant reimburses Landlord for all reasonable costs plus up to a 15% admin fee.
items.quote: as to any maintenance, repair, or replacement described in the immediately foregoing phrase which necessitate(s) entry into or onto the roof of the Premises (exclusive of the maintenance, repair and replacement of the HVAC System), the Common Areas, and/or another tenantâs premises, Landlord shall be responsible for causing such maintenance, repair, and/or replacements to be made, but Tenant shall be responsible for all commercially reasonable costs incurred by Landlord

items.summary: Landlord performs repairs requiring entry into Common Areas but bills Tenant for the reasonable costs plus admin fee; Tenant must also keep any loading/truck dock areas it uses clean.
items.quote: Landlord shall be responsible for causing such maintenance, repair, and/or replacements to be made, but Tenant shall be responsible for all commercially reasonable costs incurred by Landlord in conjunction therewith... In addition, Tenant shall maintain and keep clean any loading platform, truck dock and maneuvering space used by Tenant

items.summary: Landlord is responsible for complying with governmental regulations affecting structural portions of the Premises (i.e., structural elements remain Landlord's domain, distinct from tenant's non-structural obligations).
items.quote: Landlord shall comply with all Governmental Regulations which affect the Shopping Center, the Common Areas or the structural portions of the Premises

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for all repairs, maintenance, and alterations to the Premises not specifically assigned to Landlord elsewhere in the lease.
items.quote: Tenant shall perform all repairs, maintenance and Alterations to the Premises not specifically set forth in Section 6.2 as an obligation of Landlord and all repairs and maintenance described in Article 5 and elsewhere throughout the Lease.

items.summary: Landlord repairs the roof structure itself (not rooftop HVAC units), unless damage arises from tenant's work, fault, or default, in which case tenant is responsible.
items.quote: Landlord shall, within thirty (30) days after receiving written notice from Tenant, commence to make repairs, if necessary, to the foundation, the roof, the exterior of the perimeter demising walls, and the load-bearing structural columns and beams in the Leased Premises

items.summary: Landlord repairs the foundation and load-bearing structural columns and beams, subject to tenant-caused-damage carve-out.
items.quote: Landlord shall...commence to make repairs, if necessary, to the foundation, the roof, the exterior of the perimeter demising walls, and the load-bearing structural columns and beams in the Leased Premises, except that if those repairs or replacements arise from...repairs, installations, alterations, or improvements by or for Tenant...then Tenant shall make such repairs

items.summary: Storefront, glass, windows, doors, door frames/hardware, and trim are excluded from landlord's 'exterior of walls' obligation and are tenant's duty to repair, maintain, and replace.
items.quote: "exterior of walls" does not include the storefront, any glass, windows, window sashes or frames, doors, door frames or hardware, trim or closure devices, or any part of the interior side of perimeter walls, all of which shall be Tenant's duty to repair, maintain, and replace

items.summary: Tenant must maintain and repair all interior elements including ceilings, interior walls, interior side of perimeter walls, floor and floor coverings.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain and make all repairs and alterations of every kind with respect to the Leased Premises (including necessary replacements) to keep it in good condition (including the storefront, glass, signs, ceilings, interior walls, interior side of perimeter walls, floor, floor coverings, plumbing, electric, heating and air conditioning, sprinklers and lighting fixtures)

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for maintaining and repairing plumbing within the leased premises, including utility lines serving the premises exclusively.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain and make all repairs and alterations of every kind with respect to the Leased Premises (including necessary replacements) to keep it in good condition (including the storefront, glass, signs, ceilings, interior walls, interior side of perimeter walls, floor, floor coverings, plumbing, electric, heating and air conditioning, sprinklers and lighting fixtures)

items.summary: Tenant must repair and maintain electrical systems and fixtures within the leased premises.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain and make all repairs and alterations of every kind with respect to the Leased Premises...(including the storefront, glass, signs, ceilings, interior walls, interior side of perimeter walls, floor, floor coverings, plumbing, electric, heating and air conditioning, sprinklers and lighting fixtures)

items.summary: Tenant maintains, repairs and replaces HVAC equipment, must keep a seasonal service contract with a reputable HVAC contractor, and must repair/replace it even if damaged by casualty, vandalism, fire, or lightning (rooftop units are expressly excluded from landlord's roof obligation).
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain a service contract for the regular seasonal maintenance of the heating, ventiating and air conditioning ("HVAC") system servicing the Leased Premises with a reputable HVAC contractor at all times during the Lease. Additionally, if any air conditioning or heating equipment (or other utilty equipment) is damaged by vandalism, fire, lightning or other casualty, Tenant shall repair (and if necessary, replacel the equipment

items.summary: Tenant repairs and replaces all utility lines, apparatus, and equipment (water, gas, electricity, sewerage) from the point they exclusively serve the premises, whether inside or outside.
items.quote: From the point they serve the Leased Premises exclusively, whether located inside or outside, Tenant shall make all repairs, replacements and alterations necessary to maintain in good condition all lines, apparatus, and equipment relating to utilties (including heating, air conditioning, water, gas, electricity and sewerage).

items.summary: Tenant is responsible, at its sole expense, for compliance with codes relating to installation, maintenance, upgrading, repair or replacement of sprinkler systems, and must indemnify landlord for failures.
items.quote: comply with all present and future laws, regulations, building codes and/or fire codes applicable to the Leased Premises or to Tenant's use or occupancy or business operations, including those that relate to installation, maintenance, upgrading, repair or replacement of sprinkler systems

items.summary: Landlord has no general obligation to maintain/improve the shopping center or common areas absent express agreement, but costs for water tank/machinery maintenance are passed through as Common Area Costs billed to tenants; tenant must also keep common area free of shopping carts.
items.section: Art. 9 (AS IS clause); Art. 10 utilities cost clause
items.quote: all costs and expenses for the maintenance, repair, replacement and installation of tanks, electric costs, machinery, apparatus and facilties shall be included in Yearly Common Area Costs

items.summary: Landlord retains exclusive right to use/control the parking lot and common areas but no express repair obligation is stated beyond the general AS-IS disclaimer; tenant accepted the parking lot as-is with no landlord repair obligation absent express agreement.
items.quote: Tenant accepts the Leased Premises and all the equipment, apparatus, plumbing, heating, air conditioning, electric, water, waste disposal and other systems relating thereto and the parking lot and the other Common Area of the Shopping Center "AS IS"

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for its signs as part of the general repair/maintenance obligation covering storefront, glass, and signs.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain and make all repairs and alterations of every kind with respect to the Leased Premises (including necessary replacements) to keep it in good condition (including the storefront, glass, signs, ceilings, interior walls...)

items.summary: All work other than that expressly performed by Landlord under the lease must be done by Tenant.
items.quote: All work other than that to be performed by Landlord, if any, shall be accomplished by Tenant.

items.summary: Landlord maintains, operates, repairs and replaces the Common Area and exterior portions of the Shopping Center, though costs are generally passed through to tenants as Common Area Costs.
items.quote: Landlord shall maintain, operate, repair and replace, or cause to be maintained, operated, repaired and replaced, the Common Area and all exterior portions of the Shopping Center in an economical and efficient manner

items.summary: Parking areas are part of the Common Area which Landlord maintains and repairs (with periodic maintenance/repairs to parking areas noted).
items.quote: maintenance or repairs to the parking areas located within the Tenant Protected Area or other portions of the Shopping Center

items.summary: Landlord repairs damage from subsidence or structural/latent defects and maintains/replaces structural portions of Tenant's Building (foundations, exterior walls, columns, floor slab, roof structure) at its own cost, not as a CAM charge.
items.quote: repair (or cause to be repaired) all damage to the Premises or Common Area caused by subsidence or other structural or latent defects; maintain in good condition and repair and replace, if reasonably necessary, the roofing, canopy, gutters, downspouts, exterior painting, wiring, plumbing, pipes, conduits and equipment which serve the Premises but are not located within the interior thereof and all structural portions of Tenant's Building

items.summary: Landlord maintains, repairs and replaces the roofing and roof structure at its sole cost, outside of Common Area Costs.
items.quote: maintain in good condition and repair and replace, if reasonably necessary, the roofing, canopy, gutters, downspouts, exterior painting, wiring, plumbing, pipes, conduits and equipment which serve the Premises but are not located within the interior thereof

items.summary: Landlord maintains/replaces plumbing, pipes and conduits serving the Premises that are outside the interior, while Tenant is responsible for plumbing/systems located within and exclusively serving the interior.
items.quote: maintain in good condition and repair and replace, if reasonably necessary, ... wiring, plumbing, pipes, conduits and equipment which serve the Premises but are not located within the interior thereof

items.summary: Landlord maintains/replaces wiring and equipment serving the Premises that is outside the interior, while Tenant handles electrical systems within the interior serving only the Premises.
items.quote: maintain in good condition and repair and replace, if reasonably necessary, the roofing, canopy, gutters, downspouts, exterior painting, wiring, plumbing, pipes, conduits and equipment which serve the Premises but are not located within the interior thereof

items.summary: Landlord is responsible for the storefront structure (excluding plate glass) as part of the building's structural portions, but Tenant must replace any glass damaged or broken in the Premises (except for casualty-related glass damage, which is Landlord's responsibility).
items.quote: store front (excluding plate glass); floor slab; roof structure and all elements supporting the floor or roof

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for maintenance and repair of all parts of the HVAC system.
items.quote: Tenant will be responsible for maintenance and repair of all parts of the HVAC system.

items.summary: Tenant must maintain the interior of Tenant's Building in good order and perform all repairs and maintenance to the interior not required to be made by Landlord, including interior building systems and exterior doors.
items.quote: Tenant shall maintain the interior of Tenant's Building in good order and shall perform such repairs and maintenance to the interior of Tenant's Building as are not required under this Section to be made by Landlord.

items.summary: Landlord makes repairs/changes required due to its own negligence, casualty, vandalism, or breach; Tenant reimburses Landlord for certain repair costs upon demand in some circumstances.
items.quote: and (subject to Sections 8.7 and 22) make all repairs and changes to the Premises or Common Area required by reason of the negligence of the Landlord, its employees, agents, contractors or other tenants or occupants of the Shopping Center, fire or other casualty, vandalism or any breach by Landlord of any provision of this Lease.

items.summary: Landlord repairs the roof, but tenant bears cost if damage results from tenant's negligence or willful misconduct; landlord also has right to install signage on roof.
items.quote: Landlord shall be responsible for repairs upon (i) the roof and exterior walls of the Prentises and (ii) utility systems... Tenant shall be responsible for the costs and expenses of any maintenance, repair and replacement of any glass and dOOlways, and any roofing and exterior walls if the necessity therefor should be the result of Tenant's negligence or willful misconduct

items.summary: Landlord is responsible for exterior wall repairs, except tenant pays if damage is caused by tenant's negligence or misconduct.
items.quote: Landlord shall be responsible for repairs upon (i) the roof and exterior walls of the Prentises and (ii) utility systems, including water, sewer, electric and gas, which are located outside of the Premises.

items.summary: Landlord is responsible for repairs to utility systems (water, sewer, electric, gas) located outside the Premises.
items.quote: Landlord shall be responsible for repairs upon ... utility systems, including water, sewer, electric and gas, which are located outside of the Premises.

items.summary: Tenant must maintain and repair all doors (interior and exterior) and all plate glass and window glass at its own expense.
items.quote: Tenant... shall maintain and repair all doors (exterior and interior) and all plate glass and window glass, effecting all such repairs and replacements at its own expense

items.summary: Tenant must maintain and replace electrical installations within the interior of the Premises.
items.quote: Tenant shall keep the interior of the Premises in good repair, maintaining and replacing, when necessary, all electrical, plumbing, heating, ventilating, air conditioning ("HVAC") and other mechanical installations

items.summary: Tenant must maintain and replace plumbing installations within the interior of the Premises.
items.quote: Tenant shall keep the interior of the Premises in good repair, maintaining and replacing, when necessary, all electrical, plumbing, heating, ventilating, air conditioning ("HVAC") and other mechanical installations

items.summary: Tenant maintains, repairs and replaces HVAC at its own expense, must keep a standard maintenance agreement in force with semiannual inspections, but landlord may step in and bill tenant if tenant fails to act.
items.quote: Tenant, at its sole cost and expense, agrees to keep in force a standard maintenance agreement on all HVAC equipment and to provide a copy of such maintenance agreement to Landlord. The agreement shall require a semiannual inspection of such equipment and the ducts within any such system

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for installing and maintaining fire extinguishers, life safety equipment, and related equipment per applicable laws and manufacturer recommendations.
items.quote: Tenant shall also be responsible for the installation and maintenance of all applicable fire extinguishers, life safety equipment and related equipment required by applicable law and for the periodic maintenance and servicing of same

items.summary: Tenant must paint and keep the interior of the Premises, including the storefront, in good condition and repair, and return it in good condition at lease end (normal wear excepted).
items.quote: To paint and keep the interior of the Premises, including the store front, in good condition and repair and to deliver the Premises to Landlord at the end of the Lease Term in as good condition as they were when received by Tenant, excepting only normal wear and tear and repairs required to be made by Landlord

items.summary: Tenant must contract for termite and pest extermination services for the Premises.
items.quote: To contract for termite and pest extennination services for the Premises which shall be rendered no

items.summary: Landlord maintains and repairs common area items (e.g., parking lot, landscaping, lighting) with costs passed through to tenant via CAM reconciliation.
items.quote: Parking Lot Repair (Sidewalk) 11,700.21 ... Parking Lot Maintenance 6,748.00 ... Parking Lot Lighting 12,231.72 ... Parking Lot Sweeping 32,133.31 ... Landscaping 58,148.47

items.summary: Landlord performs parking lot repair, maintenance, sweeping, and lighting, but costs are billed back to tenant through CAM charges.
items.quote: Parking Lot Repair (Sidewalk) 11,700.21 ... Parking Lot Maintenance 6,748.00 ... Parking Lot Lighting 12,231.72 ... Parking Lot Sweeping 32,133.31

items.summary: Landlord maintains landscaping but recovers the cost from tenant through CAM charges.
items.quote: Landscaping 58,148.47

items.summary: Landlord maintains signs/canopy and parking lot signage, with costs recovered from tenant via CAM.
items.quote: Signs & Directories (Canopy) 895.00 ... Parking Lot Other (Signage) 463.00

items.summary: General building repairs (Gen Bldg R&M) performed by landlord are passed through to tenant as CAM expense.
items.quote: Gen Bldg R&M Repairs 8,840.00

items.summary: Landlord maintains and repairs all portions of the roof, roof structures and supports, including damage to Tenant's ceiling from leaks, at its sole cost.
items.quote: keep or cause to be kept ... and shall promptly repair any damage thereto: (i) all portions of the roof, roof structures and supports (and including Tenant's interior ceiling damaged from leaking), and all structural portions of the Premises

items.summary: Landlord is responsible for all structural portions of the Premises including foundation, structural supports, exterior/load bearing walls, floors (not floor coverings), gutters and downspouts.
items.quote: all structural portions of the Premises, including but not limited to, the foundation and structural supports, exterior and load bearing walls, floors (but not floor coverings), gutters, and downspouts

items.summary: Landlord maintains all utilities up to the point they exclusively serve the Premises, whether located inside or outside.
items.quote: all utilities to the point they serve the Premises exclusively, whether located inside or outside the Premises

items.summary: Landlord maintains the Common Area and Common Improvements (including snow/ice removal), but this cost is billable back to Tenant via CAM under Sec. 7.02.
items.quote: the Common Area and Common Improvements of the Landlord's Parcel, and subject to the REA, the remaining Common Areas and Common Improvements of the Shopping Center, including the removal of snow and ice therefrom

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for maintaining and repairing the heating and air conditioning system, including exterior components, unless it falls under Landlord's structural obligations.
items.quote: heating and air conditioning system (including any exterior components thereof, subject to Landlord's obligations pursuant to Section 6.01 hereinabove)

items.summary: Tenant maintains interior plumbing systems, subject to Landlord's separate structural/utility obligations under Sec. 6.01.
items.quote: Interior plumbing and electrical systems (subject to Landlord's obligations pursuant to Section 6.01 hereinabove)

items.summary: Tenant maintains interior electrical systems, subject to Landlord's separate structural/utility obligations under Sec. 6.01.
items.quote: Interior plumbing and electrical systems (subject to Landlord's obligations pursuant to Section 6.01 hereinabove)

items.summary: Tenant is responsible for keeping the plate glass, storefront, and interior/exterior doors in neat, clean, and repaired condition.
items.quote: the plate glass, storefront and interior and exterior doors

items.summary: Tenant maintains the non-structural interior improvements and the interior of the building generally, except for landlord's latent construction defects.
items.quote: those non-structural, interior improvements in the Premises ... and the interior of the building generally, except for latent defects due to faUlty construction by Landlord, its agents, contractors or subcontractors

items.summary: Tenant may only make structural or exterior alterations with Landlord's prior written consent, and must repair any damage from removal of its own trade fixtures/shelves.
items.quote: Any exterior or structural alterations and remodelings may be made by Tenant only with the prior written consent of Landlord.

items.summary: In emergencies, Tenant may perform repairs normally done by Landlord to prevent material damage, with Landlord reimbursing costs for which Tenant is not responsible.
items.quote: Tenant may, in the event of an emergency, immediately make those repairs ordinarily performed by Landlord which are reasonably necessary to prevent material adverse damage to the Premises
